questionHtml:
A wide variety of organic compounds are used by living organisms.

Explain how carbohydrates are transported from plant leaves.
markschemeHtml:
1. translocation/movement by mass flow;
  2. in phloem sieve tubes;
  3. sieve plates/pores in end walls/lack of organelles allows flow (of sap);
  4. carbohydrates (principally) transported as sucrose;
  5. (sucrose/glucose/sugar/carbohydrate) loaded (into phloem) by active transport;
  6. loading/pumping in (of sugars) by companion cells;
  7. high solute concentration generated (at the source);
  8. water enters by osmosis (due to the high solute concentration);
  9. hydrostatic pressure increased/high hydrostatic pressure generated;
  10. pressure gradient causes flow (from source to sink);
  11. leaves are a source because carbohydrates are made there;
  12. transport to the sink where carbohydrates are used/stored;

meta.title: Explain how carbohydrates are transported from plant leaves.
breadcrumbLabels: 9.2 Transport in the phloem of plants
